question_text: An electric bell works using an electromagnet. Explain how the electromagnet causes the bell to ring repeatedly.
correct_answer: When current flows, the electromagnet becomes magnetic and attracts an iron armature, which hits the bell. As the armature moves, it breaks the electrical circuit (opens a switch), so the electromagnet loses its magnetism. The armature springs back, closing the circuit again. This cycle repeats, causing repeated bell strikes.
explanation: An electric bell is an example of a device that uses an electromagnet as an automatic switch. The key is the feedback loop: the electromagnet switches itself on and off by moving the very armature it attracts, creating continuous ringing.
